The PlayStation 5 Disc Edition is Sony's original ninth-generation home console featuring an Ultra HD Blu-ray Disc drive. It introduced a custom ultra-high-speed SSD, hardware-accelerated ray tracing, Tempest 3D AudioTech, and support for up to 120 FPS gameplay. The built-in disc drive allows players to enjoy both physical and digital games while also serving as a 4K Ultra HD Blu-ray player. Production ended after the launch of the redesigned PlayStation 5 Slim models.

The PlayStation 5 Digital Edition is the disc-free version of Sony's original PlayStation 5. It delivers identical gaming performance to the Disc Edition but relies entirely on digital downloads and the PlayStation Store for software purchases. The streamlined design appealed to players embracing an all-digital library. Production ended following the introduction of the PlayStation 5 Slim Digital Edition.

The PlayStation 5 Slim Disc Edition is a redesigned, smaller version of the PlayStation 5 featuring the same performance as the original model in a more compact chassis. It includes a detachable Ultra HD Blu-ray Disc drive, increased internal storage, and improved serviceability while maintaining full compatibility with the PlayStation 5 ecosystem. It is the standard retail PlayStation 5 model sold by Sony today.

The PlayStation 5 Slim Digital Edition is the all-digital version of the redesigned PlayStation 5. It features the same compact hardware and upgraded internal storage as the Slim Disc Edition but ships without a disc drive. Players can later purchase and attach Sony's official Disc Drive accessory if they wish to use physical games and Blu-ray movies.

The PlayStation 5 Pro is Sony's premium ninth-generation console designed to deliver enhanced graphics performance and more consistent high frame rates than the standard PlayStation 5. It introduces a larger GPU, advanced ray tracing capabilities, AI-driven PlayStation Spectral Super Resolution (PSSR), and improved rendering performance while remaining fully compatible with the existing PlayStation 5 game library. Unlike previous PlayStation models, it ships without a disc drive, which can be added separately using Sony's optional Disc Drive accessory.

Compatibility: PlayStation 5 Slim Digital Edition, PlayStation 5 Pro
Summary: The PlayStation Disc Drive is an official detachable Ultra HD Blu-ray Disc drive developed by Sony for the PlayStation 5 Slim Digital Edition and PlayStation 5 Pro. It enables playback of physical PlayStation 5 and supported PlayStation 4 game discs, as well as Ultra HD Blu-ray, Blu-ray, and DVD movies. The drive connects seamlessly to compatible consoles, allowing players to add physical media support to otherwise digital-only systems without replacing the console.

Summary: The DualSense Wireless Controller is Sony's standard controller for the PlayStation 5 and one of the console's defining innovations. It introduced advanced haptic feedback, adaptive triggers, an integrated microphone, a built-in speaker, motion sensing, and a redesigned ergonomic form factor that significantly enhances immersion during gameplay. In addition to the PlayStation 5, the controller is compatible with PC, Mac, and supported Android and Apple devices via USB or Bluetooth, making it one of Sony's most versatile first-party accessories.

Compatibility: PlayStation 5, PC, Mac, PULSE Elite Wireless Headset, PULSE Explore Wireless Earbuds
Summary: The PlayStation Link USB Adapter is Sony's official wireless USB transmitter for devices that support PlayStation Link technology. It delivers ultra-low latency, lossless wireless audio between compatible PlayStation Link accessories and supported devices, providing a stable, high-quality audio experience for gaming and voice communication. Multiple adapters can be paired with the same compatible headset or earbuds, allowing users to switch seamlessly between devices such as a PlayStation 5 and a PC.
